rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,4-alkoxydimerization of aryl alkenes with alcohols to give symmetric and asymmetric 1,4-dialkoxybutanes
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of styrene to a radical cation that initiates dimerization; a second anodic oxidation converts a β-alkoxy carbon radical to a carbocation; cathode reduces H+ to H2
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes styrene by SET to a radical cation (I), which adds to a second styrene to form distonic cation-radical II; methanol traps the cation end giving β-alkoxy radical III; a second anodic SET oxidizes III to carbocation IV, which is trapped by a second equivalent of methanol to deliver the 1,4-dialkoxybutane; protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: Initial anodic single-electron oxidation of styrene (onset ~+1.713 V)

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: 2 equiv BHT fully suppressed product formation; HRMS detected BHT adduct V of radical intermediate III

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene
probe_reagents.observation: No ring-opened product observed; instead 1,2-dimethoxylation byproduct A (53%) and dimer 21 (21%) formed, attributed to fast oxidation of the radical to a cation before cyclopropyl ring-opening

probe_reagents.name: styrene (cyclic voltammetry)
probe_reagents.observation: Onset oxidation potential ~+1.713 V vs reference, confirming feasibility of anodic SET to form a cation/radical cation

notes: Pyrrole-, pyridine-, and furan-substituted alkenes failed, likely due to competing oxidation of the heteroarene. With the cyclopropyl probe, the absence of ring-opening is rationalized by rapid second SET (radical→cation) outcompeting the radical clock, which is itself mechanistic evidence for the radical-polar crossover step (III→IV). H2O as nucleophile gave only 12% yield; aniline, benzotriazole, AcOH, TMSCN, NH4CN were unsuccessful nucleophiles.
